name: pravo-grammatika
description: Grammar of the legal text on Russian legal practice — the third element of the trivium (grammar · logic · rhetoric). Checks the FORM OF EXPRESSION: whether a clause says unambiguously what was intended or admits more than one reading. Core: syntactic ambiguity and punctuation that cause contract-interpretation disputes — the last-antecedent problem, the scope of and/or, 'including but not limited to', negation and exceptions, deadline boundaries, anaphora and cross-references, deontic modality (may/shall/must), and typography that changes meaning. Modes: ambiguity, punctuation, norm, rewrite. The key criterion of every finding is HOW the legal result changes. Never resolves ambiguity silently — shows both readings, leaves the choice to the author. Validity is out of scope (flagged to pravo-logika), persuasiveness too (flagged to pravo-ritorika); statutes and case law are flagged for human verification.

interface.shortDescription: Legal-grammar skill (RU): check a clause's FORM for syntactic ambiguity, punctuation that changes rights, and/or scope, deadline boundaries, anaphora, deontic modality — always by how the legal result changes.
